description: List all supplier invoices entered within a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Invoice, i.Branch, i.InvoiceDate, i.DueDate, i.Reference, i.OrigInvValue, i.Currency, i.InvoiceStatus FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY i.InvoiceDate DESC, i.Supplier
embedding_text: Returns a list of accounts-payable supplier invoices from the ApInvoice table whose InvoiceDate falls between two dates. Joined with ApSupplier for supplier names. Useful for invoice registers, period audit, daily/weekly purchase invoice listings.
example_questions: Show me all supplier invoices from January 2025
AP invoices for the last 6 months
Show supplier invoices for last quarter
List all invoices from last 3 months
AP invoice register for this year

description: All purchase invoices booked against a specific supplier
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, i.DueDate, i.OrigInvValue, i.Currency, i.MthInvBal1 AS CurrentBalance, i.InvoiceStatus, i.Reference FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.Supplier = %(supplier)s ORDER BY i.InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns every invoice raised on a specific supplier code from ApInvoice, including original value, current outstanding balance (MthInvBal1) and status. Useful for vendor account statements.
example_questions: All purchase invoices for supplier SUP001
Vendor ABC ke saare invoices dikhao
Show me invoices booked against supplier 1001

description: Purchase invoices for a specific AP branch within a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Branch, b.Description AS BranchName, i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, i.OrigInvValue, i.Currency, i.InvoiceStatus FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApBranch b ON b.Branch = i.Branch LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.Branch = %(branch)s AND i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY i.InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns AP invoices filtered by Branch from ApInvoice joined with ApBranch for branch description. Useful for branch-wise purchase reporting.
example_questions: Purchase invoices from main branch in January 2025
Show supplier invoices for branch A between two dates

description: AP invoices that have no purchase order linked in ApInvoiceRegPo
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Invoice, i.Branch, i.InvoiceDate, i.OrigInvValue, i.Currency, i.Reference FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ApInvoiceRegPo p WHERE p.Supplier = i.Supplier AND p.Invoice = i.Invoice) ORDER BY i.OrigInvValue DESC
embedding_text: Returns supplier invoices that have no linked purchase order in ApInvoiceRegPo. Useful for non-PO spend detection, control violations, and 3-way matching exceptions.
example_questions: Invoices without a purchase order number
Which invoices have no linked PO?

description: High-value AP invoices above a user-defined threshold
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Invoice, i.Branch, i.InvoiceDate, i.OrigInvValue, i.Currency, i.InvoiceStatus, i.Reference FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.OrigInvValue >= %(threshold)s AND i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY i.OrigInvValue DESC
embedding_text: Returns AP invoices whose original invoice value is greater than or equal to a threshold, within a date range. Useful for material-value audit and large-spend reviews.
example_questions: Show large supplier invoices greater than 10000

description: AP invoices where settlement discount was taken (DiscActiveFlag = Y)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, i.DueDate, i.DiscountDate, i.OrigInvValue, i.OrigDiscValue, i.PaymDiscValue, i.DiscActiveFlag, i.Currency FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.DiscActiveFlag = 'Y' AND i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY i.OrigDiscValue DESC
embedding_text: Returns invoices where DiscActiveFlag = 'Y' indicating settlement/cash discount was availed. Useful for cash discount tracking and supplier negotiation review.
example_questions: Invoices where discount was taken last month
Discount taken vs not taken on purchase invoices

description: AP ageing buckets - current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days based on DueDate
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Currency, SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, i.DueDate, GETDATE()) <= 0 THEN i.MthInvBal1 ELSE 0 END) AS NotDue, SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, i.DueDate, GETDATE()) BETWEEN 1 AND 30 THEN i.MthInvBal1 ELSE 0 END) AS Days_1_30, SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, i.DueDate, GETDATE()) BETWEEN 31 AND 60 THEN i.MthInvBal1 ELSE 0 END) AS Days_31_60, SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, i.DueDate, GETDATE()) BETWEEN 61 AND 90 THEN i.MthInvBal1 ELSE 0 END) AS Days_61_90, SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, i.DueDate, GETDATE()) > 90 THEN i.MthInvBal1 ELSE 0 END) AS Over90, SUM(i.MthInvBal1) AS TotalOutstanding FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.MthInvBal1 <> 0 GROUP BY i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Currency ORDER BY TotalOutstanding DESC
embedding_text: Returns AP ageing in standard buckets (Not Due, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+) using DATEDIFF on DueDate vs GETDATE(). Outstanding amount taken from MthInvBal1. Grouped by supplier and currency.
example_questions: Show ageing report for supplier invoices

description: All supplier invoices whose due date is in the past and still have balance
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, i.DueDate, DATEDIFF(day, i.DueDate, GETDATE()) AS DaysOverdue, i.OrigInvValue, i.MthInvBal1 AS Outstanding, i.Currency FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.MthInvBal1 <> 0 AND i.DueDate < GETDATE() ORDER BY DaysOverdue DESC
embedding_text: Returns supplier invoices where DueDate is before today and outstanding balance (MthInvBal1) is non-zero. Sorted by days overdue descending. Useful for collection-call lists from the AP side.
example_questions: Overdue supplier invoices as of today
Which AP invoices are past due?

description: AP invoices due in the next N days for cash planning
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, i.DueDate, DATEDIFF(day, GETDATE(), i.DueDate) AS DaysToDue, i.MthInvBal1 AS Outstanding, i.Currency FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.MthInvBal1 <> 0 AND i.DueDate BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(day, %(days_ahead)s, GETDATE()) ORDER BY i.DueDate
embedding_text: Returns AP invoices whose DueDate falls between today and today + N days. Useful for short-term cash forecasting and payment run preparation.
example_questions: Invoices due in the next 7 days for cash planning
Which supplier invoices are due next 30 days?

description: Top suppliers ranked by total overdue outstanding amount
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, COUNT(*) AS OverdueInvoiceCount, SUM(i.MthInvBal1) AS TotalOverdue, MAX(DATEDIFF(day, i.DueDate, GETDATE())) AS OldestDaysOverdue, s.Currency FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.MthInvBal1 <> 0 AND i.DueDate < GETDATE() GROUP BY i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, s.Currency ORDER BY TotalOverdue DESC
embedding_text: Returns top N suppliers ranked by total overdue amount. Includes count of overdue invoices and the oldest overdue age. Useful for prioritising payment runs and vendor risk reviews.
example_questions: Suppliers with the largest overdue amount

description: Suppliers with no purchase activity in the last N months
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s s.Supplier, s.SupplierName, s.LastPurchDate, s.CurrentBalance, s.Currency, DATEDIFF(day, s.LastPurchDate, GETDATE()) AS DaysSinceLastPurchase FROM ApSupplier s WHERE (s.LastPurchDate IS NULL OR s.LastPurchDate < DATEADD(month, -%(months)s, GETDATE())) ORDER BY s.LastPurchDate ASC
embedding_text: Returns suppliers whose LastPurchDate is older than N months ago (or null), suggesting inactive or dormant vendor records. Useful for vendor master cleansing.
example_questions: Suppliers with zero activity in last 6 months
Show inactive vendors (no purchase last year)

description: AP ageing buckets aggregated by supplier class/group
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s s.SupplierClass, COUNT(DISTINCT i.Supplier) AS Suppliers, SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, i.DueDate, GETDATE()) <= 0 THEN i.MthInvBal1 ELSE 0 END) AS NotDue, SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, i.DueDate, GETDATE()) BETWEEN 1 AND 30 THEN i.MthInvBal1 ELSE 0 END) AS Days_1_30, SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, i.DueDate, GETDATE()) BETWEEN 31 AND 60 THEN i.MthInvBal1 ELSE 0 END) AS Days_31_60, SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, i.DueDate, GETDATE()) BETWEEN 61 AND 90 THEN i.MthInvBal1 ELSE 0 END) AS Days_61_90, SUM(CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(day, i.DueDate, GETDATE()) > 90 THEN i.MthInvBal1 ELSE 0 END) AS Over90, SUM(i.MthInvBal1) AS TotalOutstanding FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.MthInvBal1 <> 0 GROUP BY s.SupplierClass ORDER BY TotalOutstanding DESC
embedding_text: AP ageing aggregated at SupplierClass level. Helps in viewing exposure by vendor segment (e.g. raw material, services).

description: Payment history (allocations) for a specific supplier
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s p.Supplier, s.SupplierName, p.Invoice, p.JournalDate, p.Journal, p.PaymentNumber, p.PaymentReference, p.TrnType, p.TrnValue FROM ApInvoicePay p LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = p.Supplier WHERE p.Supplier = %(supplier)s AND p.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY p.JournalDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns all payment/allocation entries from ApInvoicePay for a given supplier within a date window. Shows how cheques/EFTs and journals were applied against invoices.
example_questions: Show all payments made to vendor ABC in 2024

description: Summary of payments by payment type (cheque, EFT, manual etc.) from ApCheckIss
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.PaymentType, COUNT(*) AS NumPayments, SUM(c.ChequeValue) AS TotalValue, c.Currency FROM ApCheckIss c WHERE c.ChequeDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND c.VoidStatus <> 'V' GROUP BY c.PaymentType, c.Currency ORDER BY TotalValue DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates non-voided payments from ApCheckIss by PaymentType (typically C=Cheque, E=EFT, M=Manual). Useful for showing the channel mix of supplier payments.
example_questions: Payment method summary cheque vs EFT vs cash
What percentage of payments were by cheque last quarter?
Breakdown of supplier payments by method

description: Discounts availed vs discounts lost on supplier invoices
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s SUM(CASE WHEN i.DiscActiveFlag = 'Y' THEN i.OrigDiscValue ELSE 0 END) AS DiscountAvailed, SUM(CASE WHEN i.DiscActiveFlag <> 'Y' AND i.OrigDiscValue > 0 THEN i.OrigDiscValue ELSE 0 END) AS DiscountLost, SUM(i.OrigDiscValue) AS TotalDiscountOffered, COUNT(CASE WHEN i.DiscActiveFlag = 'Y' THEN 1 END) AS InvoicesAvailed, COUNT(CASE WHEN i.DiscActiveFlag <> 'Y' AND i.OrigDiscValue > 0 THEN 1 END) AS InvoicesLost FROM ApInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s
embedding_text: Compares discounts taken (DiscActiveFlag='Y') vs discounts forfeited where OrigDiscValue was offered but not availed. Useful for showing leakage of early-payment discounts.
example_questions: How much early payment discount did we miss?

description: Average days from invoice date to payment date per supplier
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s p.Supplier, s.SupplierName, COUNT(*) AS InvoicesPaid, AVG(CAST(DATEDIFF(day, i.InvoiceDate, p.JournalDate) AS DECIMAL(10,2))) AS AvgPaymentDays, SUM(p.TrnValue) AS TotalPaid FROM ApInvoicePay p INNER JOIN ApInvoice i ON i.Supplier = p.Supplier AND i.Invoice = p.Invoice LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = p.Supplier WHERE p.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND p.TrnType IN ('P','C') GROUP BY p.Supplier, s.SupplierName HAVING COUNT(*) >= %(min_invoices)s ORDER BY AvgPaymentDays ASC
embedding_text: Computes average days between InvoiceDate and the payment JournalDate per supplier, indicating effective DPO at the vendor level.
example_questions: Which suppliers get paid the fastest?

description: Supplier credit balances / unallocated payments still sitting against the account
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s s.Supplier, s.SupplierName, s.CurrentBalance, s.Currency, s.LastChequeDate, s.LastChequeVal FROM ApSupplier s WHERE s.CurrentBalance < 0 ORDER BY s.CurrentBalance ASC
embedding_text: Returns suppliers whose CurrentBalance is negative, indicating unallocated payments or credits on account. Useful for highlighting payments not applied to invoices.

description: Invoices where early payment discount was taken in a specific period
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, i.DiscountDate, i.OrigInvValue, i.OrigDiscValue AS DiscountValue, i.PaymDiscValue, i.Currency FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.DiscActiveFlag = 'Y' AND i.OrigDiscValue > 0 AND i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY i.OrigDiscValue DESC
embedding_text: Returns line-level detail of invoices where settlement/early-payment discounts were successfully availed. Useful for savings-tracking dashboards.
example_questions: Early payment discounts taken in January

description: Withholding tax deducted per supplier within a period
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s w.Supplier, s.SupplierName, COUNT(*) AS NumTransactions, SUM(w.WithTaxableValue) AS TaxableValue, SUM(w.WithTaxValue) AS WHTValue, AVG(w.WithTaxRate) AS AvgWHTRate FROM ApWithholdingTax w LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = w.Supplier WHERE w.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY w.Supplier, s.SupplierName ORDER BY WHTValue DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates withholding tax deducted per supplier from ApWithholdingTax. Includes taxable value, tax value and average rate. Useful for WHT/TDS deduction reports.
example_questions: WHT deducted per supplier in Q1
Withholding tax summary for vendor SUP001

description: WHT entries pending certificate issuance (Status not closed)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s w.Supplier, s.SupplierName, w.Invoice, w.InvoiceDate, w.WithTaxCode, w.WithTaxValue, w.Status FROM ApWithholdingTax w LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = w.Supplier WHERE (w.Status IS NULL OR w.Status NOT IN ('C','I')) AND w.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY w.InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns withholding tax records whose Status is not yet Closed/Issued, indicating that the certificate to the supplier is still pending.
example_questions: WHT certificates pending to be issued
Which suppliers have pending WHT certificates?

description: Compare WHT rates applied vs statutory rates in AdmWithholdingTax
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s w.WithTaxCode, wt.Description, wt.CurTaxRate AS StatutoryRate, AVG(w.WithTaxRate) AS AvgAppliedRate, COUNT(*) AS NumTransactions, SUM(CASE WHEN ABS(w.WithTaxRate - wt.CurTaxRate) > 0.01 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS NumDeviations FROM ApWithholdingTax w LEFT JOIN AdmWithholdingTax wt ON wt.WithTaxCode = w.WithTaxCode WHERE w.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY w.WithTaxCode, wt.Description, wt.CurTaxRate ORDER BY NumDeviations DESC
embedding_text: Compares effective WHT rate applied (from ApWithholdingTax) against the statutory rate defined in AdmWithholdingTax. Highlights records where rate differs.
example_questions: WHT rates applied vs statutory rates
Are we deducting correct WHT percentage?

description: Suppliers flagged for WHT but with no WHT entries in the period
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s s.Supplier, s.SupplierName, s.WithTaxCode, s.WithTaxId, s.LastPurchDate FROM ApSupplier s WHERE s.WithTaxCode IS NOT NULL AND s.WithTaxCode <> '' AND s.WithTaxCode <> ' ' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM ApWithholdingTax w WHERE w.Supplier = s.Supplier AND w.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s) ORDER BY s.LastPurchDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns suppliers configured with a WithTaxCode but having no WHT transactions in the period. Useful to surface possible non-deduction issues.
example_questions: List vendors where WHT was not deducted

description: WHT liability summary by tax code for the period
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s w.WithTaxCode, wt.Description, COUNT(*) AS NumTransactions, SUM(w.WithTaxableValue) AS TotalTaxable, SUM(w.WithTaxValue) AS TotalWHT FROM ApWithholdingTax w LEFT JOIN AdmWithholdingTax wt ON wt.WithTaxCode = w.WithTaxCode WHERE w.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY w.WithTaxCode, wt.Description ORDER BY TotalWHT DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates WHT deducted by tax code. Useful for compiling the WHT payable schedule by category before remitting to government.
example_questions: Total withholding tax payable for last month

description: WHT cheques deposited (paid out) from ApWithholdTax payment table
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s w.PaymYear, w.PaymentPeriod, w.WithTaxCode, w.Cheque, w.PaymDate, w.Bank, SUM(w.WithTaxValue) AS WHTDeposited, COUNT(*) AS NumEntries FROM ApWithholdTax w WHERE w.PaymDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY w.PaymYear, w.PaymentPeriod, w.WithTaxCode, w.Cheque, w.PaymDate, w.Bank ORDER BY w.PaymDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns WHT amounts deposited (paid out) from ApWithholdTax aggregated by cheque/payment, for the period. Useful for reconciling WHT remitted to authorities.
example_questions: TDS payments to authorities last quarter

description: Top N suppliers ranked by total invoice value in a period
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, COUNT(*) AS InvoiceCount, SUM(i.OrigInvValue) AS TotalSpend, AVG(i.OrigInvValue) AS AvgInvoiceValue, s.Currency FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, s.Currency ORDER BY TotalSpend DESC
embedding_text: Ranks suppliers by total invoice value in the period. Useful for spend analytics, vendor concentration, and Pareto analysis.
example_questions: Top 10 suppliers by spend this month
Suppliers with highest purchase volume in 2025

description: Month-by-month total purchase value trend
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.InvoiceYear, i.InvoiceMonth, COUNT(*) AS InvoiceCount, SUM(i.OrigInvValue) AS TotalPurchases FROM ApInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY i.InvoiceYear, i.InvoiceMonth ORDER BY i.InvoiceYear, i.InvoiceMonth
embedding_text: Returns total purchase invoice value per InvoiceYear/InvoiceMonth. Useful for line/bar chart of purchase trends.
example_questions: Purchase value by month for last year
Trend of AP invoices by month

description: Purchase value by supplier class as a proxy for product/spend category
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s s.SupplierClass, COUNT(DISTINCT i.Supplier) AS UniqueSuppliers, COUNT(*) AS InvoiceCount, SUM(i.OrigInvValue) AS TotalSpend FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY s.SupplierClass ORDER BY TotalSpend DESC
embedding_text: Returns purchase spend rolled up by SupplierClass (used as the spend/product category proxy in Syspro Core Financials). For pure inventory-product breakdown, the inventory module would be needed.
example_questions: Which product categories had highest spend?

description: Suppliers whose purchase volume has declined year-over-year using ApSupplier YTD vs PYTD columns
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s s.Supplier, s.SupplierName, (s.PurchValMth1 + s.PurchValMth2 + s.PurchValMth3 + s.PurchValMth4 + s.PurchValMth5 + s.PurchValMth6 + s.PurchValMth7 + s.PurchValMth8 + s.PurchValMth9 + s.PurchValMth10 + s.PurchValMth11 + s.PurchValMth12) AS CurrentYearPurchases, (s.PyrMerchVal + s.PyrFreightVal + s.PyrOtherVal) AS PreviousYearPurchases, CASE WHEN (s.PyrMerchVal + s.PyrFreightVal + s.PyrOtherVal) <> 0 THEN ((s.PurchValMth1 + s.PurchValMth2 + s.PurchValMth3 + s.PurchValMth4 + s.PurchValMth5 + s.PurchValMth6 + s.PurchValMth7 + s.PurchValMth8 + s.PurchValMth9 + s.PurchValMth10 + s.PurchValMth11 + s.PurchValMth12) - (s.PyrMerchVal + s.PyrFreightVal + s.PyrOtherVal)) * 100.0 / (s.PyrMerchVal + s.PyrFreightVal + s.PyrOtherVal) ELSE NULL END AS PctChange FROM ApSupplier s WHERE (s.PyrMerchVal + s.PyrFreightVal + s.PyrOtherVal) > 0 AND ((s.PurchValMth1 + s.PurchValMth2 + s.PurchValMth3 + s.PurchValMth4 + s.PurchValMth5 + s.PurchValMth6 + s.PurchValMth7 + s.PurchValMth8 + s.PurchValMth9 + s.PurchValMth10 + s.PurchValMth11 + s.PurchValMth12) - (s.PyrMerchVal + s.PyrFreightVal + s.PyrOtherVal)) * 100.0 / (s.PyrMerchVal + s.PyrFreightVal + s.PyrOtherVal) <= -%(decline_pct)s ORDER BY PctChange ASC
embedding_text: Identifies suppliers whose current-year purchases (sum of PurchValMth1..12 on ApSupplier) declined by at least N% versus previous year (PyrMerchVal + PyrFreightVal + PyrOtherVal).
example_questions: Show vendors whose purchase dropped 20% vs last year

description: Identify suppliers with similar names/types that could potentially be consolidated
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s s.SupplierClass, s.Currency, COUNT(*) AS SupplierCount, SUM(s.CurrentBalance) AS TotalBalance, MIN(s.Supplier) AS SampleSupplier1, MAX(s.Supplier) AS SampleSupplier2 FROM ApSupplier s WHERE s.SupplierClass IS NOT NULL AND s.SupplierClass <> '' GROUP BY s.SupplierClass, s.Currency HAVING COUNT(*) >= %(min_count)s ORDER BY SupplierCount DESC
embedding_text: Groups suppliers by class and currency, flagging groups with multiple vendors that may indicate consolidation opportunities. A finer analysis on supplier name similarity would need a separate fuzzy-match step.

description: Actual purchase value variance vs prior period at supplier level
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, SUM(CASE WHEN i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(curr_start)s AND %(curr_end)s THEN i.OrigInvValue ELSE 0 END) AS CurrentPeriod, SUM(CASE WHEN i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(prior_start)s AND %(prior_end)s THEN i.OrigInvValue ELSE 0 END) AS PriorPeriod, SUM(CASE WHEN i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(curr_start)s AND %(curr_end)s THEN i.OrigInvValue ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(prior_start)s AND %(prior_end)s THEN i.OrigInvValue ELSE 0 END) AS Variance FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(prior_start)s AND %(curr_end)s GROUP BY i.Supplier, s.SupplierName ORDER BY ABS(SUM(CASE WHEN i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(curr_start)s AND %(curr_end)s THEN i.OrigInvValue ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(prior_start)s AND %(prior_end)s THEN i.OrigInvValue ELSE 0 END)) DESC
embedding_text: Calculates purchase variance per supplier between two date ranges (current vs prior period). True actual-vs-budget at supplier level would require a supplier-level budget which Syspro does not store natively; this query uses prior period as the benchmark.
example_questions: Purchase variance: actual vs prior month
Variance per supplier vs last quarter

description: Reconcile AP sub-ledger balance (ApControl) against the AP control GL account balance
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.FinYear, c.FinPeriod, (c.CurBalance1 + c.CurBalance2 + c.CurBalance3) AS SubledgerBalance, (SELECT SUM(g.CurrentBalance) FROM GenMaster g WHERE g.ControlAccFlag = 'A') AS GLControlAccountBalance, (c.CurBalance1 + c.CurBalance2 + c.CurBalance3) - (SELECT SUM(g.CurrentBalance) FROM GenMaster g WHERE g.ControlAccFlag = 'A') AS Difference FROM ApControl c WHERE c.CtlFlag = 'A '
embedding_text: Compares AP sub-ledger balance (CurBalance1+2+3 in ApControl) with the sum of GL accounts whose ControlAccFlag='A' (AP control accounts). Useful for month-end reconciliation.
example_questions: AP sub-ledger vs GL control account reconciliation
Check if AP sub-ledger matches general ledger

description: List AP-source journals in GenJournalCtl that are not yet posted
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s jc.GlYear, jc.GlPeriod, jc.GlJournal, jc.JournalDate, jc.Source, jc.NumOfEntries, jc.DebitAmount, jc.CreditAmount, jc.JnlStatus, jc.Operator FROM GenJournalCtl jc WHERE jc.Source IN ('A','P') AND jc.JnlStatus <> 'P' ORDER BY jc.GlYear DESC, jc.GlPeriod DESC, jc.GlJournal DESC
embedding_text: Returns journals originating from AP (Source 'A' or 'P') whose JnlStatus is not yet Posted ('P'). Useful for closing-checklist exception lists.
example_questions: List AP journals that are not yet posted

description: Recurring journal entries in GL (used for AP-side recurring postings)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s rc.JournalCode, rc.Reference, rc.Notation, rc.PostFrequency, rc.NextPostYear, rc.NextPostPeriod, rc.LastYearPosted, rc.LastPeriodPosted, rc.DebitAmount, rc.CreditAmount, rc.Source FROM GenRecJnlCtl rc WHERE rc.Source IN ('A','P') ORDER BY rc.NextPostYear, rc.NextPostPeriod
embedding_text: Returns recurring journal templates in GenRecJnlCtl with Source='A'/'P' (AP-side). Useful for review of automated postings.

description: Show current AP period and month-end status flags for current year
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.FinYear, c.FinPeriod, c.CurYrMthStat1, c.CurYrMthStat2, c.CurYrMthStat3, c.CurYrMthStat4, c.CurYrMthStat5, c.CurYrMthStat6, c.CurYrMthStat7, c.CurYrMthStat8, c.CurYrMthStat9, c.CurYrMthStat10, c.CurYrMthStat11, c.CurYrMthStat12, c.CurYrMthStat13 FROM ApControl c WHERE c.CtlFlag = 'A '
embedding_text: Returns the AP control flags showing each period's status for the current financial year (O=Open, C=Closed). Useful for period-end status dashboards.
example_questions: AP period closing status is period 1 closed?
Which AP periods are still open?

description: Aggregate AP-side journals by JournalSource for a period
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s jc.Source, jc.JournalSource, COUNT(*) AS NumJournals, SUM(jc.NumOfEntries) AS TotalEntries, SUM(jc.DebitAmount) AS TotalDebit, SUM(jc.CreditAmount) AS TotalCredit FROM GenJournalCtl jc WHERE jc.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND jc.Source IN ('A','P') GROUP BY jc.Source, jc.JournalSource ORDER BY TotalDebit DESC
embedding_text: Groups AP journals by JournalSource code in the period. Useful for breakdown by sub-module (invoices, payments, adjustments).
example_questions: Journal entry source breakdown for AP

description: Debit and credit notes register from ApCrDrReg
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s 'See ApCrDrReg' AS Note, c.* FROM ApCrDrReg c WHERE 1=1 ORDER BY (SELECT NULL)
embedding_text: Returns rows from ApCrDrReg, the AP debit and credit note register. NOTE: column list returned dynamically as the ApCrDrReg structure varies by Syspro version. Use SELECT * for full payload.
example_questions: Debit notes register for last month

description: Intercompany AP cheques/payments from ApInterCoChkReg
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s 'See ApInterCoChkReg' AS Note, c.* FROM ApInterCoChkReg c WHERE 1=1 ORDER BY (SELECT NULL)
embedding_text: Returns intercompany cheque register entries from ApInterCoChkReg. Useful for intercompany AP transaction review.

description: Foreign currency AP revaluation entries from ApInvoiceReval
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s r.Supplier, s.SupplierName, r.Invoice, r.JournalDate, r.TrnYear, r.TrnMonth, r.BalRevalued, r.RevalValue, r.PostCurrency FROM ApInvoiceReval r LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = r.Supplier WHERE r.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY ABS(r.RevalValue) DESC
embedding_text: Returns foreign currency revaluation entries for AP invoices showing balance revalued and resulting gain/loss (RevalValue). Useful for FX gain/loss audit.

description: Invoices in the AP register that are pending approval
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s r.Supplier, s.SupplierName, r.Invoice, r.Branch, r.InvoiceDate, r.EntryDate, r.InvoiceValue, r.InvRegnStatus, r.AuthorisingPerson, r.FollowUpDate FROM ApInvoiceReg r LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = r.Supplier WHERE r.InvRegnStatus NOT IN ('A','P') ORDER BY r.EntryDate ASC
embedding_text: Returns invoices in ApInvoiceReg whose InvRegnStatus is not yet 'A' (approved) or 'P' (posted). Useful for tracking invoices awaiting manager approval.

description: Detect possible duplicate AP invoices by supplier + value + date
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Reference, i.OrigInvValue, i.Currency, COUNT(*) AS DuplicateCount, MIN(i.Invoice) AS FirstInvoice, MAX(i.Invoice) AS LastInvoice, MIN(i.InvoiceDate) AS FirstDate, MAX(i.InvoiceDate) AS LastDate FROM ApInvoice i LEFT JOIN ApSupplier s ON s.Supplier = i.Supplier WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND i.Reference IS NOT NULL AND i.Reference <> '' GROUP BY i.Supplier, s.SupplierName, i.Reference, i.OrigInvValue, i.Currency HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY DuplicateCount DESC, i.OrigInvValue DESC
embedding_text: Returns groups of AP invoices that share the same supplier, vendor reference number, value and currency, suggesting a possible duplicate booking.

description: Customer (sales) invoices within a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, i.Invoice, i.DocumentType, i.InvoiceDate, i.SalesOrder, i.Branch, i.Salesperson, i.OrigDiscValue, i.TaxPortion, i.InvoiceBal1, i.PostCurrency FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer WHERE i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY i.InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns AR sales invoices from ArInvoice within a date range. Joined to ArCustomer for the customer name. Useful for sales invoice listings and audit registers.
example_questions: Show me AR invoices in Q1

description: All invoices for a specific customer
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, i.Invoice, i.DocumentType, i.InvoiceDate, i.SalesOrder, i.OrigDiscValue, i.TaxPortion, i.InvoiceBal1 AS Outstanding, i.PostCurrency FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer WHERE i.Customer = %(customer)s ORDER BY i.InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns every invoice raised on a specific customer code, sorted newest first, with outstanding balance in InvoiceBal1.
example_questions: Show all customer invoices for customer CUST001

description: Sales invoices by AR branch within a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Branch, i.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, i.OrigDiscValue, i.TaxPortion, i.InvoiceBal1, i.PostCurrency FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer WHERE i.Branch = %(branch)s AND i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY i.InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns AR invoices for a specific branch within a date range. Useful for branch-wise sales reporting.

description: Credit notes issued to customers within a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, i.Invoice, i.DocumentType, i.InvoiceDate, i.OrigDiscValue, i.TaxPortion, i.InvoiceBal1, i.PostCurrency FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer WHERE i.DocumentType = 'C' AND i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY i.InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns credit notes (DocumentType='C') raised on customers within a date range. Useful for returns analysis.

description: Sales invoices with discount higher than a threshold
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, i.OrigDiscValue, s.MerchandiseValue, CASE WHEN s.MerchandiseValue > 0 THEN i.OrigDiscValue * 100.0 / s.MerchandiseValue ELSE 0 END AS DiscountPct FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer LEFT JOIN ArTrnSummary s ON s.Invoice = i.Invoice WHERE i.OrigDiscValue >= %(min_discount)s AND i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY i.OrigDiscValue DESC
embedding_text: Returns invoices where OrigDiscValue exceeds a threshold, computing percentage discount where merchandise value is available from ArTrnSummary.
example_questions: List invoices with discount more than 10000

description: Sales invoices filtered by salesperson code
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s i.Salesperson, i.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, i.Invoice, i.InvoiceDate, i.OrigDiscValue, i.TaxPortion, i.InvoiceBal1, i.PostCurrency FROM ArInvoice i LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = i.Customer WHERE i.Salesperson = %(salesperson)s AND i.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY i.InvoiceDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns AR invoices linked to a specific salesperson code from ArInvoice.Salesperson. Useful for salesperson productivity reviews.

description: Customer ageing report using ArCustomerBal pre-computed buckets
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s b.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, c.Currency, b.ValCurrentInv AS Current, b.Val30daysInv AS Days30, b.Val60daysInv AS Days60, b.Val90daysInv AS Days90, b.Val120daysInv AS Days120Plus, (b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) AS TotalBalance FROM ArCustomerBal b LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = b.Customer WHERE (b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) <> 0 ORDER BY (b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) DESC
embedding_text: Returns customer ageing using the pre-computed ageing columns on ArCustomerBal (ValCurrentInv, Val30daysInv, Val60daysInv, Val90daysInv, Val120daysInv) sorted by total balance descending.

description: Customers with overdue balances (anything past 30+ days)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s b.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, c.Currency, c.CreditLimit, (b.Val30daysInv + b.Val60daysInv + b.Val90daysInv + b.Val120daysInv) AS TotalOverdue, b.Val120daysInv AS OldestBucket FROM ArCustomerBal b LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = b.Customer WHERE (b.Val30daysInv + b.Val60daysInv + b.Val90daysInv + b.Val120daysInv) > 0 ORDER BY TotalOverdue DESC
embedding_text: Returns customers whose summed overdue buckets (>30 days) are positive. Useful for collection priority lists.

description: DSO (Days Sales Outstanding) trend by month using ArCustomerBal monthly sales and current AR balance
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s YEAR(c.CurYrMthEnd1) AS Year, m.MonthNum, m.SalesInMonth, (SELECT SUM(b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) FROM ArCustomerBal b) AS CurrentAR, CASE WHEN m.SalesInMonth > 0 THEN ((SELECT SUM(b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) FROM ArCustomerBal b) / m.SalesInMonth) * 30.0 ELSE NULL END AS DSO FROM ArControl c CROSS APPLY (VALUES (1, c.MtdSales1), (2, c.MtdSales2), (3, c.MtdSales3)) AS m(MonthNum, SalesInMonth) WHERE c.CtlFlag = 'A '
embedding_text: Calculates a simple DSO trend using ArControl monthly sales (MtdSales1..3) and current AR. NOTE: ArControl exposes the three most recent open months; for a longer trend the GenHistory approach should be used.

description: Customers whose current balance exceeds their credit limit
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, c.Currency, c.CreditLimit, (b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) AS CurrentBalance, (b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) - c.CreditLimit AS OverLimit, c.OutstOrdVal FROM ArCustomer c INNER JOIN ArCustomerBal b ON b.Customer = c.Customer WHERE c.CreditLimit > 0 AND (b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) > c.CreditLimit ORDER BY OverLimit DESC
embedding_text: Returns customers whose ArCustomerBal current balance exceeds the configured CreditLimit on ArCustomer. Useful for credit-risk dashboards.

description: Customers currently on hold (CustomerOnHold flag)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, c.CustomerOnHold, c.CreditStatus, c.PaymentStatus, c.Currency, c.CreditLimit, (b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) AS CurrentBalance, c.DateLastSale FROM ArCustomer c LEFT JOIN ArCustomerBal b ON b.Customer = c.Customer WHERE c.CustomerOnHold = 'Y' OR c.CreditStatus = 'H' ORDER BY (b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) DESC
embedding_text: Returns customers where CustomerOnHold='Y' or CreditStatus='H' indicating they are on credit hold and cannot transact.

description: Collection Effectiveness Index using opening AR + sales - closing AR over period sales
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.FinYear, c.FinPeriod, (c.MtdSales1 + c.MtdSales2 + c.MtdSales3) AS TotalSales, (c.MtdPayments1 + c.MtdPayments2 + c.MtdPayments3) AS TotalCollections, (c.ArBalance1 + c.ArBalance2 + c.ArBalance3) AS ClosingAR, CASE WHEN (c.OpeningBalance1 + c.OpeningBalance2 + c.OpeningBalance3 + c.MtdSales1 + c.MtdSales2 + c.MtdSales3) > 0 THEN (c.MtdPayments1 + c.MtdPayments2 + c.MtdPayments3) * 100.0 / (c.OpeningBalance1 + c.OpeningBalance2 + c.OpeningBalance3 + c.MtdSales1 + c.MtdSales2 + c.MtdSales3) ELSE NULL END AS CollectionEffectivenessPct FROM ArControl c WHERE c.CtlFlag = 'A '
embedding_text: Calculates Collection Effectiveness Index from ArControl summary balances. CEI = Collections / (Opening AR + Sales) * 100. Higher is better.
example_questions: Collection effectiveness index for last month

description: Customer payment receipts from ArPayHistory in a period
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s p.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, COUNT(*) AS ReceiptCount, SUM(p.PayValue) AS TotalReceived, SUM(p.LocalPayValue) AS TotalLocalCurrency, MIN(p.PayDate) AS FirstReceipt, MAX(p.PayDate) AS LastReceipt FROM ArPayHistory p LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = p.Customer WHERE p.PayDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY p.Customer, c.Name ORDER BY TotalLocalCurrency DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates customer receipt history from ArPayHistory by customer for a date range. Useful for collection summaries.

description: Customer receipts grouped by payment type from ArCshJnlDet
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s d.PaymentType, COUNT(*) AS ReceiptCount, SUM(d.ChequeValue) AS TotalValue FROM ArCshJnlDet d WHERE d.ChequeDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY d.PaymentType ORDER BY TotalValue DESC
embedding_text: Summarises AR customer receipts by PaymentType (typically C=Cash, Q=Cheque, R=Credit Card, E=EFT) from ArCshJnlDet.
example_questions: Payment type summary cash cheque card EFT

description: Customers with negative balances indicating unallocated receipts on account
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s b.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, c.Currency, (b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) AS NegativeBalance, c.DateLastPay FROM ArCustomerBal b LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = b.Customer WHERE (b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) < 0 ORDER BY (b.CurrentBalance1 + b.CurrentBalance2 + b.CurrentBalance3) ASC
embedding_text: Returns customers whose current balance is negative across all three sub-balances, suggesting unallocated receipts or over-payments sitting on account.

description: Discounts allowed to customers from ArInvoicePay
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s p.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, COUNT(*) AS NumInvoices, SUM(p.DiscValue) AS TotalDiscount, SUM(p.TrnValue) AS TotalPayment FROM ArInvoicePay p LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = p.Customer WHERE p.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND p.DiscValue > 0 GROUP BY p.Customer, c.Name ORDER BY TotalDiscount DESC
embedding_text: Returns discounts granted to customers from ArInvoicePay.DiscValue grouped by customer. Useful for monitoring discount expense per customer.
example_questions: Discounts allowed to customers last month
Which customers got the most discounts?

description: Bad-debt write-offs recorded as adjustments in ArInvoicePay (TrnType in known write-off codes)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s p.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, p.Invoice, p.JournalDate, p.TrnType, p.TrnValue, p.Reference FROM ArInvoicePay p LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = p.Customer WHERE p.TrnType IN ('W','B') AND p.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY p.JournalDate DESC, ABS(p.TrnValue) DESC
embedding_text: Returns AR invoice-pay transactions whose TrnType indicates a write-off ('W' for write-off, 'B' for bad debt - adjust to your installation's convention). Useful for bad-debt monitoring.
example_questions: Bad debts written off this year

description: Expected receipts in coming weeks based on outstanding invoices and terms
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s YEAR(i.InvoiceDate) AS InvYear, MONTH(i.InvoiceDate) AS InvMonth, COUNT(*) AS InvoiceCount, SUM(i.InvoiceBal1) AS TotalOutstanding, MIN(i.InvoiceDate) AS EarliestInvoice, MAX(i.InvoiceDate) AS LatestInvoice FROM ArInvoice i WHERE i.InvoiceBal1 <> 0 GROUP BY YEAR(i.InvoiceDate), MONTH(i.InvoiceDate) ORDER BY InvYear DESC, InvMonth DESC
embedding_text: Provides a basic receipts forecast by aggregating outstanding ArInvoice balances by invoice month. For full forecasting, customer terms code would be applied to project the due date.

description: Top customers by revenue (NetSalesValue) from ArTrnDetail in a period
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, COUNT(DISTINCT t.Invoice) AS InvoiceCount, SUM(t.NetSalesValue) AS Revenue, SUM(t.CostValue) AS Cost, SUM(t.NetSalesValue - t.CostValue) AS GrossProfit, c.Currency FROM ArTrnDetail t LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = t.Customer WHERE t.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.DocumentType = 'I' GROUP BY t.Customer, c.Name, c.Currency ORDER BY Revenue DESC
embedding_text: Ranks customers by NetSalesValue from ArTrnDetail for invoices (DocumentType='I'). Also computes cost and gross profit per customer.

description: Sales by stock code (product) from ArTrnDetail
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.StockCode, t.ProductClass, SUM(t.QtyInvoiced) AS QtySold, SUM(t.NetSalesValue) AS Revenue, SUM(t.CostValue) AS Cost, SUM(t.NetSalesValue - t.CostValue) AS GrossProfit FROM ArTrnDetail t WHERE t.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.DocumentType = 'I' GROUP BY t.StockCode, t.ProductClass ORDER BY Revenue DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates ArTrnDetail by StockCode + ProductClass showing qty, revenue, cost and gross profit per product.
example_questions: Sales by product line / stock code

description: Month-on-month sales comparison this year vs last year
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s YEAR(t.InvoiceDate) AS SalesYear, MONTH(t.InvoiceDate) AS SalesMonth, SUM(t.NetSalesValue) AS Revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT t.Invoice) AS InvoiceCount FROM ArTrnDetail t WHERE t.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.DocumentType = 'I' GROUP BY YEAR(t.InvoiceDate), MONTH(t.InvoiceDate) ORDER BY SalesYear, SalesMonth
embedding_text: Monthly aggregation of revenue from ArTrnDetail across a multi-year date range to enable year-over-year comparison.
example_questions: Monthly sales comparison this year vs last year

description: Sales by geography (Area from ArTrnDetail / ArCustomer state)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Area, c.State, c.Nationality, COUNT(DISTINCT t.Customer) AS CustomerCount, SUM(t.NetSalesValue) AS Revenue FROM ArTrnDetail t LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = t.Customer WHERE t.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.DocumentType = 'I' GROUP BY t.Area, c.State, c.Nationality ORDER BY Revenue DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates sales by geographic dimensions: Area from ArTrnDetail and State/Nationality from ArCustomer. Useful for regional sales reporting.
example_questions: Sales by geography area state country

description: Sales totals by salesperson with commission summary
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Salesperson, COUNT(DISTINCT t.Customer) AS UniqueCustomers, COUNT(DISTINCT t.Invoice) AS Invoices, SUM(t.NetSalesValue) AS Revenue, SUM(t.NetSalesValue - t.CostValue) AS GrossProfit, SUM(s.CommissionValue) AS Commission FROM ArTrnDetail t LEFT JOIN ArTrnSummary s ON s.TrnYear = t.TrnYear AND s.TrnMonth = t.TrnMonth AND s.Register = t.Register AND s.Invoice = t.Invoice AND s.SummaryLine = t.SummaryLine WHERE t.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.DocumentType = 'I' GROUP BY t.Salesperson ORDER BY Revenue DESC
embedding_text: Returns sales productivity per salesperson by aggregating ArTrnDetail. Commission from ArTrnSummary.CommissionValue.

description: Gross profit % by customer using ArTrnDetail
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Customer, c.Name AS CustomerName, SUM(t.NetSalesValue) AS Revenue, SUM(t.CostValue) AS Cost, SUM(t.NetSalesValue - t.CostValue) AS GrossProfit, CASE WHEN SUM(t.NetSalesValue) > 0 THEN SUM(t.NetSalesValue - t.CostValue) * 100.0 / SUM(t.NetSalesValue) ELSE 0 END AS GpPct FROM ArTrnDetail t LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = t.Customer WHERE t.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.DocumentType = 'I' GROUP BY t.Customer, c.Name HAVING SUM(t.NetSalesValue) > 0 ORDER BY GrossProfit DESC
embedding_text: Computes gross profit and GP% per customer by summing NetSalesValue and CostValue from ArTrnDetail.

description: Sales returns as % of total sales (credit notes vs invoices)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s SUM(CASE WHEN t.DocumentType = 'I' THEN t.NetSalesValue ELSE 0 END) AS GrossSales, SUM(CASE WHEN t.DocumentType = 'C' THEN ABS(t.NetSalesValue) ELSE 0 END) AS Returns, CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN t.DocumentType = 'I' THEN t.NetSalesValue ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN SUM(CASE WHEN t.DocumentType = 'C' THEN ABS(t.NetSalesValue) ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / SUM(CASE WHEN t.DocumentType = 'I' THEN t.NetSalesValue ELSE 0 END) ELSE 0 END AS ReturnsPct FROM ArTrnDetail t WHERE t.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s
embedding_text: Calculates total returns (credit notes) as a percentage of gross sales. Helpful for product-quality and order accuracy KPIs.
example_questions: Sales returns as percentage of total sales

description: Profit margin by product class
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.ProductClass, SUM(t.NetSalesValue) AS Revenue, SUM(t.CostValue) AS Cost, SUM(t.NetSalesValue - t.CostValue) AS GrossProfit, CASE WHEN SUM(t.NetSalesValue) > 0 THEN SUM(t.NetSalesValue - t.CostValue) * 100.0 / SUM(t.NetSalesValue) ELSE 0 END AS MarginPct FROM ArTrnDetail t WHERE t.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.DocumentType = 'I' GROUP BY t.ProductClass ORDER BY GrossProfit DESC
embedding_text: Returns revenue, cost, GP and margin% by ProductClass from ArTrnDetail. Useful for product portfolio profitability analysis.
example_questions: Which product lines have best margin?

description: New customers added in a period using DateCustAdded
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.Customer, c.Name, c.DateCustAdded, c.Currency, c.CreditLimit, c.Salesperson, c.Branch, c.Area FROM ArCustomer c WHERE c.DateCustAdded BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY c.DateCustAdded DESC
embedding_text: Lists customers whose DateCustAdded falls in the supplied range. Useful for new-customer acquisition tracking.

description: Customers with no sales activity in the last N months
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.Customer, c.Name, c.DateLastSale, c.DateLastPay, c.Currency, DATEDIFF(day, c.DateLastSale, GETDATE()) AS DaysSinceLastSale FROM ArCustomer c WHERE (c.DateLastSale IS NULL OR c.DateLastSale < DATEADD(month, -%(months)s, GETDATE())) ORDER BY c.DateLastSale ASC
embedding_text: Returns customers whose DateLastSale is older than N months, indicating dormant accounts for win-back campaigns.
example_questions: Inactive customers no purchase last 6 months

description: Customer lifetime value (cumulative net sales) for top customers
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Customer, c.Name, MIN(t.InvoiceDate) AS FirstInvoice, MAX(t.InvoiceDate) AS LastInvoice, COUNT(DISTINCT t.Invoice) AS LifetimeInvoices, SUM(t.NetSalesValue) AS LifetimeRevenue, SUM(t.NetSalesValue - t.CostValue) AS LifetimeProfit FROM ArTrnDetail t LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = t.Customer WHERE t.DocumentType IN ('I','C') GROUP BY t.Customer, c.Name ORDER BY LifetimeRevenue DESC
embedding_text: Computes customer lifetime value by aggregating all ArTrnDetail history per customer. Returns top N by lifetime revenue.
example_questions: Customer lifetime value for top 10 customers

description: Average order/invoice value per customer in a period
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Customer, c.Name, COUNT(DISTINCT t.Invoice) AS InvoiceCount, SUM(t.NetSalesValue) AS Revenue, CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT t.Invoice) > 0 THEN SUM(t.NetSalesValue) / COUNT(DISTINCT t.Invoice) ELSE 0 END AS AvgOrderValue FROM ArTrnDetail t LEFT JOIN ArCustomer c ON c.Customer = t.Customer WHERE t.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.DocumentType = 'I' GROUP BY t.Customer, c.Name ORDER BY AvgOrderValue DESC
embedding_text: Computes Average Order Value per customer = Revenue / unique invoices, within a date range.

description: Repeat purchase rate (% of customers with >1 invoice in period)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s COUNT(DISTINCT t.Customer) AS TotalCustomers, SUM(CASE WHEN counts.InvoiceCount > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS RepeatCustomers, CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT t.Customer) > 0 THEN SUM(CASE WHEN counts.InvoiceCount > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT t.Customer) ELSE 0 END AS RepeatRatePct FROM (SELECT Customer, COUNT(DISTINCT Invoice) AS InvoiceCount FROM ArTrnDetail WHERE InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND DocumentType = 'I' GROUP BY Customer) counts CROSS JOIN ArTrnDetail t WHERE t.Customer = counts.Customer AND t.InvoiceDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.DocumentType = 'I'
embedding_text: Computes repeat purchase rate = % of customers that placed more than one invoice in the period. Useful for retention KPI.

description: Reconcile AR sub-ledger (ArControl) against AR control GL accounts
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.FinYear, c.FinPeriod, (c.ArBalance1 + c.ArBalance2 + c.ArBalance3) AS SubledgerBalance, (SELECT SUM(g.CurrentBalance) FROM GenMaster g WHERE g.ControlAccFlag = 'R') AS GLControlAccountBalance, (c.ArBalance1 + c.ArBalance2 + c.ArBalance3) - (SELECT SUM(g.CurrentBalance) FROM GenMaster g WHERE g.ControlAccFlag = 'R') AS Difference FROM ArControl c WHERE c.CtlFlag = 'A '
embedding_text: Compares AR sub-ledger (ArBalance1+2+3 in ArControl) with sum of GL accounts flagged as AR control accounts (ControlAccFlag='R'). Useful for month-end recon.
example_questions: AR sub-ledger vs GL control reconciliation

description: AR-source journals that are not yet posted
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s jc.GlYear, jc.GlPeriod, jc.GlJournal, jc.JournalDate, jc.Source, jc.NumOfEntries, jc.DebitAmount, jc.CreditAmount, jc.JnlStatus, jc.Operator FROM GenJournalCtl jc WHERE jc.Source IN ('R','S') AND jc.JnlStatus <> 'P' ORDER BY jc.GlYear DESC, jc.GlPeriod DESC
embedding_text: Returns journals originating from AR (Source 'R' or 'S' for AR/Sales) that are not posted.

description: AR period closing status for current year
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.FinYear, c.FinPeriod, c.CurYrMthStat1, c.CurYrMthStat2, c.CurYrMthStat3, c.CurYrMthStat4, c.CurYrMthStat5, c.CurYrMthStat6, c.CurYrMthStat7, c.CurYrMthStat8, c.CurYrMthStat9, c.CurYrMthStat10, c.CurYrMthStat11, c.CurYrMthStat12, c.CurYrMthStat13 FROM ArControl c WHERE c.CtlFlag = 'A '
embedding_text: Returns AR control month-status flags for the current year showing which AR periods are still open (O) vs closed (C).

description: Provision for doubtful debts based on ageing buckets
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s 'CalcSummary' AS Bucket, SUM(b.ValCurrentInv) AS CurrentTotal, SUM(b.ValCurrentInv) * %(rate_current)s / 100.0 AS Provision_Current, SUM(b.Val30daysInv) AS Days30Total, SUM(b.Val30daysInv) * %(rate_30)s / 100.0 AS Provision_30, SUM(b.Val60daysInv) AS Days60Total, SUM(b.Val60daysInv) * %(rate_60)s / 100.0 AS Provision_60, SUM(b.Val90daysInv) AS Days90Total, SUM(b.Val90daysInv) * %(rate_90)s / 100.0 AS Provision_90, SUM(b.Val120daysInv) AS Days120Total, SUM(b.Val120daysInv) * %(rate_120)s / 100.0 AS Provision_120, (SUM(b.ValCurrentInv) * %(rate_current)s / 100.0 + SUM(b.Val30daysInv) * %(rate_30)s / 100.0 + SUM(b.Val60daysInv) * %(rate_60)s / 100.0 + SUM(b.Val90daysInv) * %(rate_90)s / 100.0 + SUM(b.Val120daysInv) * %(rate_120)s / 100.0) AS TotalProvision FROM ArCustomerBal b
embedding_text: Computes provision for doubtful debts using user-configurable provisioning percentages applied to each ageing bucket from ArCustomerBal.

description: Trial balance with debit/credit balances per GL account as of a year/period
sql: SELECT m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.ExpenseType, m.GlGroup, ISNULL(h.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS OpeningBalance, ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) AS ClosingBalance, CASE WHEN ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) >= 0 THEN ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) ELSE 0 END AS Debit, CASE WHEN ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) < 0 THEN -ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) ELSE 0 END AS Credit FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s ORDER BY m.GlCode
embedding_text: Returns trial balance from GenMaster joined with GenHistory closing balances per period. Splits balances into debit and credit columns. Used for period-end TB, audit, and balance sheet preparation.
example_questions: Show me TB for period 1 of 2025
Generate trial balance for current period

description: Profit & Loss summary for one period (month) grouped by income/expense type
sql: SELECT m.AccountType, m.ExpenseType, m.GlGroup, m.GlCode, m.Description, ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 - h.BeginYearBalance WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 - h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 - h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 - h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 - h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 - h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 - h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 - h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 - h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 - h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 - h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.ClosingBalPer11 END, 0) AS PeriodMovement FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE m.AccountType IN ('R','E') ORDER BY m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Generates monthly P&L by computing the movement between consecutive period closing balances in GenHistory for revenue (R) and expense (E) accounts. Joined to GenMaster for descriptions.
example_questions: Profit & Loss statement for last month

description: Profit & Loss for a quarter (3 consecutive periods) by computing the closing balance delta
sql: SELECT m.AccountType, m.ExpenseType, m.GlGroup, m.GlCode, m.Description, ISNULL((CASE %(end_period)s WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END) - (CASE %(start_period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.BeginYearBalance WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 END), 0) AS QuarterMovement FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE m.AccountType IN ('R','E') ORDER BY m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Quarterly P&L using GenHistory period closing balances. Computes delta from start of quarter to end of quarter for revenue and expense GL accounts. Useful for board reporting and quarterly results.

description: Balance sheet showing closing balances for asset, liability, and capital accounts
sql: SELECT m.AccountType, CASE m.AccountType WHEN 'A' THEN 'Asset' WHEN 'L' THEN 'Liability' WHEN 'C' THEN 'Capital' END AS AccountTypeDesc, m.GlGroup, g.Description AS GroupDescription, m.GlCode, m.Description, ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) AS ClosingBalance FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s LEFT JOIN GenGroups g ON g.Company = m.Company AND g.GlGroup = m.GlGroup WHERE m.AccountType IN ('A','L','C') ORDER BY m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Balance sheet from GenMaster filtered to asset (A), liability (L), and capital (C) account types, with closing balances pulled from GenHistory for the requested period. Joined to GenGroups for group descriptions. Used for monthly/yearly financial position reporting.
example_questions: Balance sheet as of year end
Show me balance sheet for period 12 2025

description: Cash flow statement using the direct method based on Cash Book transactions
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN t.TrnType IN ('R') THEN 'Operating Inflows' WHEN t.TrnType IN ('P','C') THEN 'Operating Outflows' WHEN t.TrnType = 'A' THEN 'Adjustments' WHEN t.TrnType = 'D' THEN 'Deposits' ELSE 'Other' END AS FlowCategory, t.TrnType, t.Bank, COUNT(*) AS TransactionCount, SUM(t.TrnValue) AS TotalValue, SUM(t.CurrencyValue) AS LocalValue FROM CshTransactions t WHERE t.TrnDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND ISNULL(t.VoidFlag, 'N') <> 'Y' GROUP BY t.TrnType, t.Bank ORDER BY t.TrnType, t.Bank
embedding_text: Direct method cash flow statement aggregating CshTransactions by transaction type (Receipt, Payment, Adjustment) over a date range. Provides bank-wise inflow/outflow breakdown. Excludes voided transactions.
example_questions: Show cash inflows and outflows for last quarter

description: Indirect cash flow building blocks: net income plus non-cash adjustments and working capital changes
sql: SELECT m.AccountType, m.ExpenseType, m.GlGroup, m.GlCode, m.Description, ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 - h.BeginYearBalance WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 - h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 - h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 - h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 - h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 - h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 - h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 - h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 - h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 - h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 - h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 - h.ClosingBalPer11 END, 0) AS PeriodMovement FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE (m.AccountType IN ('R','E') OR m.GlGroup LIKE %(working_capital_group)s) ORDER BY m.AccountType, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Indirect method cash flow inputs: period movement of P&L accounts (R, E) plus working capital movement (matched by GlGroup pattern). Caller adds back depreciation and adjustments. Reads GenHistory and GenMaster.
example_questions: Cash flow indirect method for last quarter

description: Statement of changes in equity (capital accounts) for the year
sql: SELECT m.GlCode, m.Description, m.GlGroup, ISNULL(h.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS OpeningBalance, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer1, 0) AS EndPer1, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer2, 0) AS EndPer2, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer3, 0) AS EndPer3, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer4, 0) AS EndPer4, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer5, 0) AS EndPer5, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer6, 0) AS EndPer6, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer7, 0) AS EndPer7, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer8, 0) AS EndPer8, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer9, 0) AS EndPer9, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer10, 0) AS EndPer10, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer11, 0) AS EndPer11, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer12, 0) AS EndPer12, ISNULL(h.ClosingBalPer12, 0) - ISNULL(h.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS NetMovement FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s WHERE m.AccountType = 'C' ORDER BY m.GlCode
embedding_text: Statement of changes in equity for all Capital (AccountType = 'C') GL accounts. Shows opening, period-end balances for 12 periods, and net movement over the year. Useful for shareholder reporting and year-end disclosures.
example_questions: How did our equity change in 2025?
Capital account movement for the year

description: All transactions posted to a specific GL account in a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.GlCode, m.Description AS AccountName, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.CurrencyValue, t.PostCurrency, t.Origin, t.SubModRef, t.SubModSupplier, t.SubModCustomer, t.SubModApInvoice, t.SubModArInvoice FROM GenTransaction t LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.GlCode = %(gl_code)s AND t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY t.JnlDate DESC, t.Journal DESC
embedding_text: Account-level activity from GenTransaction for a specific GlCode within a date range, joined to GenMaster for the account name. Shows source, reference, sub-module link fields. Used for account analysis and drill-down.
example_questions: Show all postings to account X
Ledger for one account between two dates

description: Highest value journal entries in a date range
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.GlCode, m.Description, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency, t.Origin FROM GenTransaction t LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY ABS(t.EntryValue) DESC
embedding_text: Returns the top N journal entries by absolute value from GenTransaction in a date range. Useful for materiality analysis, audit sampling, and unusual transaction detection.

description: Journal entry count and total value grouped by source module
sql: SELECT t.Source, COUNT(*) AS EntryCount, SUM(CASE WHEN t.EntryValue >= 0 THEN t.EntryValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalDebits, SUM(CASE WHEN t.EntryValue < 0 THEN -t.EntryValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalCredits, SUM(t.EntryValue) AS NetValue FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY t.Source ORDER BY EntryCount DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates GenTransaction entries by Source code (G=General, A=AR, P=AP, C=Cash, I=Inventory etc.) to show which modules feed the GL. Counts entries and totals debits, credits and net value.
example_questions: Journals by source module AR AP Cash
How many entries per source code?
GL postings breakdown by feeder module

description: List of recurring journal templates from GenRecJnlCtl with their detail lines
sql: SELECT c.JournalCode, c.Reference, c.Notation, c.NumOfEntries, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount, c.PostFrequency, c.NextPostYear, c.NextPostPeriod, c.LastYearPosted, c.LastPeriodPosted, c.Authorised, c.AuthorisedBy FROM GenRecJnlCtl c ORDER BY c.JournalCode
embedding_text: Returns recurring journal templates set up in GenRecJnlCtl. Shows posting frequency, last posted period and next scheduled period. Useful for month-end close planning and accrual review.

description: All transactions posted to a suspense GL account that need clearing
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.GlCode, m.Description, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency, t.SubModRef FROM GenTransaction t INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.GlCode = %(suspense_account)s AND t.JnlDate <= GETDATE() ORDER BY t.JnlDate DESC
embedding_text: Returns all postings to the suspense account from GenTransaction so they can be investigated and cleared before period close. Pass the suspense GL code as parameter.
example_questions: Show all entries posted to suspense account

description: GL journal summary by posting currency
sql: SELECT t.PostCurrency, COUNT(*) AS EntryCount, SUM(CASE WHEN t.CurrencyValue >= 0 THEN t.CurrencyValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalDebitsForeign, SUM(CASE WHEN t.CurrencyValue < 0 THEN -t.CurrencyValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalCreditsForeign, SUM(CASE WHEN t.EntryValue >= 0 THEN t.EntryValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalDebitsLocal, SUM(CASE WHEN t.EntryValue < 0 THEN -t.EntryValue ELSE 0 END) AS TotalCreditsLocal FROM GenTransaction t WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND t.PostCurrency IS NOT NULL AND t.PostCurrency <> '' GROUP BY t.PostCurrency ORDER BY EntryCount DESC
embedding_text: Summarises GenTransaction by PostCurrency showing both foreign currency value (CurrencyValue) and local value (EntryValue) totals. Used for multi-currency GL exposure reporting.

description: Open/closed status of all GL periods for the current and previous year
sql: SELECT c.Company, c.GlYear AS CurrentYear, c.GlPeriod AS CurrentPeriod, c.CurYrPrdStat1 AS Per1, c.CurYrPrdEnd1, c.CurYrPrdStat2 AS Per2, c.CurYrPrdEnd2, c.CurYrPrdStat3 AS Per3, c.CurYrPrdEnd3, c.CurYrPrdStat4 AS Per4, c.CurYrPrdEnd4, c.CurYrPrdStat5 AS Per5, c.CurYrPrdEnd5, c.CurYrPrdStat6 AS Per6, c.CurYrPrdEnd6, c.CurYrPrdStat7 AS Per7, c.CurYrPrdEnd7, c.CurYrPrdStat8 AS Per8, c.CurYrPrdEnd8, c.CurYrPrdStat9 AS Per9, c.CurYrPrdEnd9, c.CurYrPrdStat10 AS Per10, c.CurYrPrdEnd10, c.CurYrPrdStat11 AS Per11, c.CurYrPrdEnd11, c.CurYrPrdStat12 AS Per12, c.CurYrPrdEnd12 FROM GenControl c
embedding_text: Returns period status flags (O=Open, C=Closed) and period end dates for all 12 periods of the current GL year from GenControl. Used by closing checklist and audit.
example_questions: Which GL periods are still open?

description: GL journal batches that have not yet been posted
sql: SELECT c.GlYear, c.GlPeriod, c.GlJournal, c.JournalDate, c.Source, c.Operator, c.JnlStatus, c.Reference, c.Notation, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount, c.NumOfEntries, c.Authorised, c.AuthorisedBy FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.JnlStatus IN ('U','P') OR c.PostDate IS NULL ORDER BY c.GlYear DESC, c.GlPeriod DESC, c.GlJournal
embedding_text: Lists GL journal control records from GenJournalCtl that are still unposted (status U=Unposted, P=Pending) or where PostDate is null. Used in period-end closing checklist.

description: Unbalanced or error journals where debits do not equal credits
sql: SELECT c.GlYear, c.GlPeriod, c.GlJournal, c.JournalDate, c.Source, c.Operator, c.Reference, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount, c.DebitAmount - c.CreditAmount AS Imbalance, c.JnlStatus FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND c.GlPeriod = %(period)s AND ABS(c.DebitAmount - c.CreditAmount) > 0.01 ORDER BY ABS(c.DebitAmount - c.CreditAmount) DESC
embedding_text: Finds GL journal batches in GenJournalCtl where debit total differs from credit total by more than 0.01 — indicates posting errors that must be fixed before closing the period.

description: Combined period-end checklist: unposted batches, AP/AR/Cash unposted journals, and tax return status
sql: SELECT 'GL unposted journals' AS CheckItem, COUNT(*) AS PendingCount FROM GenJournalCtl WHERE GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND GlPeriod = %(period)s AND (JnlStatus IN ('U','P') OR PostDate IS NULL) UNION ALL SELECT 'AP unposted journals' AS CheckItem, COUNT(*) AS PendingCount FROM ApJnlControl WHERE FinYear = %(gl_year)s AND FinMonth = %(period)s AND JournalStatus <> 'P' UNION ALL SELECT 'Cash unposted journals' AS CheckItem, COUNT(*) AS PendingCount FROM CshJnlCtl WHERE GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND GlPeriod = %(period)s AND ISNULL(GlIntUpdated,'N') <> 'Y' UNION ALL SELECT 'Unbalanced batches' AS CheckItem, COUNT(*) AS PendingCount FROM GenJournalCtl WHERE GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND GlPeriod = %(period)s AND ABS(DebitAmount - CreditAmount) > 0.01
embedding_text: Period-end closing checklist combining unposted GL journals (GenJournalCtl), unposted AP journals (ApJnlControl), unposted Cash journals (CshJnlCtl) and unbalanced batches. One row per check showing pending count.
example_questions: Period end checklist – what is pending?

description: Year-end closing journal entries (P&L roll-up to retained earnings)
sql: SELECT t.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.RetEarnsAccount, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.Comment, t.EntryValue, t.Origin FROM GenTransaction t INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND t.Source IN ('Y','C') AND (t.Reference LIKE '%%YEAR%%END%%' OR t.Reference LIKE '%%CLOSE%%' OR t.Comment LIKE '%%year%%end%%' OR t.Comment LIKE '%%closing%%') ORDER BY t.JnlDate, t.Journal
embedding_text: Year-end closing entries — filters GenTransaction by source codes Y/C and reference/comment text indicating year-end or closing. Joined to GenMaster to show retained earnings account and account type.

description: Drill from a GL account balance to the sub-ledger transaction that created it (AP/AR/Cash/SO/Job)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.GlCode, m.Description AS GlAccount, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Origin, t.Reference, t.EntryValue, t.SubModSupplier, t.SubModCustomer, t.SubModApInvoice, t.SubModArInvoice, t.SubModCheck, t.SubModBank, t.SubModSalesOrder, t.SubModJob, t.SubModAsset, t.SubModRef, t.OriginZoomKey FROM GenTransaction t LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.GlCode = %(gl_code)s AND t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (t.SubModSupplier IS NOT NULL OR t.SubModCustomer IS NOT NULL OR t.SubModApInvoice IS NOT NULL OR t.SubModArInvoice IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY t.JnlDate DESC, t.Journal DESC
embedding_text: GL-to-sub-ledger drill: returns GenTransaction lines for a GL code along with all SubMod* link fields (supplier, customer, invoice, cheque, bank, sales order, job, asset). Use SubMod* values to navigate to the originating AP/AR/Cash/SO/Job record.
example_questions: Drill from GL balance to AR sub-ledger
Show origin of postings in GL account 200100
Find sub-ledger entry behind a GL line

description: Audit trail for a specific journal — who posted it, when, and what changed
sql: SELECT c.GlYear, c.GlPeriod, c.GlJournal, c.JournalDate, c.Source, c.JournalSource, c.Operator AS PostedByOperator, c.AuthorisedBy, c.PostedBy, c.Authorised, c.JnlStatus, c.PostDate, c.Reference, c.Notation, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount, c.NumOfEntries, r.AllocatedByOper, r.AllocatedDate, r.AllocatedByProgram, r.CalledFromProgram, r.Status, r.ClosedDate FROM GenJournalCtl c LEFT JOIN GenJournalRef r ON r.GlYear = c.GlYear AND r.GlPeriod = c.GlPeriod AND r.GlJournal = c.GlJournal WHERE c.GlJournal = %(journal)s AND c.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND c.GlPeriod = %(period)s
embedding_text: Audit trail for a single journal: combines GenJournalCtl (who posted, authorised, when, status) with GenJournalRef (who allocated the journal number, from which program, when closed). Used for forensic queries.
example_questions: Show full audit log for one journal

description: Journals that were amended or marked deleted from the data API amendment journal
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s a.RunDateTime, a.TableName, a.ChangeFlag, a.BeforeValues, a.AfterValues, a.Operator, a.SysproSource, a.ApplicationName FROM AdmDataApiAmendJnl a WHERE a.RunDateTime BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (a.TableName IN ('GenJournalCtl','GenJournalDetail','GenTransaction') OR a.ChangeFlag = 'D') ORDER BY a.RunDateTime DESC
embedding_text: Looks at AdmDataApiAmendJnl for changes against GL tables (GenJournalCtl, GenJournalDetail, GenTransaction) including deletes (ChangeFlag = 'D'). Useful for audit of amended or removed journals.
example_questions: Deleted or amended journals in last month
Were any GL entries deleted recently?

description: GL journals grouped by approval status
sql: SELECT c.Authorised, c.AuthorisedBy, COUNT(*) AS JournalCount, SUM(c.DebitAmount) AS TotalDebit, SUM(c.CreditAmount) AS TotalCredit FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s GROUP BY c.Authorised, c.AuthorisedBy ORDER BY JournalCount DESC
embedding_text: Aggregates GenJournalCtl by Authorised flag (Y/N) and AuthorisedBy operator. Shows how many journals each user authorised and the totals. Used for approval audit.

description: GL journals posted in foreign (non-base) currency
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.GlYear, c.GlPeriod, c.GlJournal, c.JournalDate, c.Source, c.Operator, c.Reference, c.Currency, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND c.Currency IS NOT NULL AND c.Currency <> '' AND c.Currency <> %(base_currency)s ORDER BY c.JournalDate DESC
embedding_text: Journals in GenJournalCtl whose Currency is set and differs from the base currency. Pass base currency (e.g. 'USD') as parameter. Used for multi-currency exposure audit.

description: Posted journals that were never authorised (control violation)
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s c.GlYear, c.GlPeriod, c.GlJournal, c.JournalDate, c.PostDate, c.Source, c.Operator, c.Reference, c.DebitAmount, c.CreditAmount, c.NumOfEntries, c.JnlStatus FROM GenJournalCtl c WHERE c.JournalDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (c.Authorised <> 'Y' OR c.Authorised IS NULL) AND c.PostDate IS NOT NULL ORDER BY c.JournalDate DESC
embedding_text: Lists GL journals that were posted (PostDate set) without authorisation (Authorised <> 'Y'). Indicates a segregation-of-duties or workflow gap. Pulled from GenJournalCtl.

description: Intercompany journal entries that need elimination during consolidation
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s t.Company AS PrimaryCompany, t.SecondaryCompany, t.GlCode, m.Description, t.JnlDate, t.Journal, t.Source, t.Reference, t.EntryValue, t.PostCurrency, t.InterCompanyTrx, t.GroupInterCompany FROM GenTransaction t LEFT JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = t.Company AND m.GlCode = t.GlCode WHERE t.JnlDate BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s AND (t.InterCompanyTrx = 'Y' OR t.GroupInterCompany = 'Y' OR t.SecondaryCompany IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY t.Company, t.SecondaryCompany, t.JnlDate
embedding_text: Returns intercompany transactions in GenTransaction (where InterCompanyTrx or GroupInterCompany flags are set). Provides the data needed to prepare elimination journals at the group consolidation level.
example_questions: Show interco journals for the period

description: Group trial balance combining all companies (uses Company field in GenMaster/GenHistory)
sql: SELECT m.Company, m.GlCode, m.Description, m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, ISNULL(h.BeginYearBalance, 0) AS OpeningBalance, ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0) AS ClosingBalance FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s ORDER BY m.Company, m.AccountType, m.GlCode
embedding_text: Group TB across all companies: combines GenMaster and GenHistory keyed by Company + GlCode. Caller can then aggregate by GL group to produce consolidated balances.
example_questions: Group trial balance for all companies

description: Group P&L and Balance Sheet roll-up at group/category level across companies
sql: SELECT m.AccountType, CASE m.AccountType WHEN 'A' THEN 'Asset' WHEN 'L' THEN 'Liability' WHEN 'C' THEN 'Capital' WHEN 'R' THEN 'Revenue' WHEN 'E' THEN 'Expense' END AS TypeDesc, m.GlGroup, g.Description AS GroupDesc, SUM(ISNULL(CASE %(period)s WHEN 1 THEN h.ClosingBalPer1 WHEN 2 THEN h.ClosingBalPer2 WHEN 3 THEN h.ClosingBalPer3 WHEN 4 THEN h.ClosingBalPer4 WHEN 5 THEN h.ClosingBalPer5 WHEN 6 THEN h.ClosingBalPer6 WHEN 7 THEN h.ClosingBalPer7 WHEN 8 THEN h.ClosingBalPer8 WHEN 9 THEN h.ClosingBalPer9 WHEN 10 THEN h.ClosingBalPer10 WHEN 11 THEN h.ClosingBalPer11 WHEN 12 THEN h.ClosingBalPer12 END, 0)) AS GroupTotal FROM GenMaster m LEFT JOIN GenHistory h ON h.Company = m.Company AND h.GlCode = m.GlCode AND h.GlYear = %(gl_year)s LEFT JOIN GenGroups g ON g.Company = m.Company AND g.GlGroup = m.GlGroup GROUP BY m.AccountType, m.GlGroup, g.Description ORDER BY m.AccountType, m.GlGroup
embedding_text: Group-level P&L and BS roll-up aggregated by AccountType and GlGroup across all companies. Joins GenMaster, GenHistory, GenGroups. Used for board-level summary statements.

description: Historical exchange rate changes for a currency
sql: SELECT TOP %(limit)s h.Currency, h.DateRateChange, h.TimeRateChange, h.ConvRate, h.MulDiv, h.Triangulated, h.TriangCurrency, h.Operator, h.ReasonForChange FROM GenHistCur h WHERE h.Currency = %(currency)s AND h.DateRateChange BETWEEN %(start_date)s AND %(end_date)s ORDER BY h.DateRateChange DESC, h.TimeRateChange DESC
embedding_text: Historical conversion rates from GenHistCur for one currency across a date range. Shows operator who changed the rate and the reason. Used for FX rate audit and reval verification.
example_questions: Show FX rate changes for EUR

description: Unrealized forex gain/loss by GL account using last revaluation rate
sql: SELECT r.Currency, r.Company, r.GlCode, m.Description, r.GlYear, r.GlPeriod, r.LastRate, l.AveExchangeRate, l.MthEndExchangeRate, l.RevaluationType, l.RevaluationDate FROM GenLastRevalRate r INNER JOIN GenMaster m ON m.Company = r.Company AND m.GlCode = r.GlCode LEFT JOIN GenRevalLog l ON l.Company = r.Company AND l.PostCurrency = r.Currency AND l.GlYear = r.GlYear AND l.GlPeriod = r.GlPeriod WHERE r.Currency = %(currency)s AND r.GlYear = %(gl_year)s AND r.GlPeriod = %(period)s ORDER BY r.GlCode
embedding_text: Joins GenLastRevalRate (rate used at last revaluation per account) with GenRevalLog (avg and month-end rate applied at that revaluation). Used to compute and review unrealized forex gain/loss positions.
example_questions: Show revaluation rates for USD accounts

description: P&L budget vs actual variance for the year by GL account
